prompt: Trace how an Express application registers middleware and routes, then dispatches an incoming request through router layers to a route handler. Cite the source files that support the path.

expected_symbols.name: createApplication
expected_symbols.why: Creates the callable application object and mixes in application behavior.

expected_symbols.name: app.use
expected_symbols.why: Registers middleware on the application router.

expected_symbols.name: app.route
expected_symbols.why: Creates a route entry through the application router.

expected_symbols.name: router
expected_symbols.why: Dispatches requests through the router stack.

expected_symbols.name: Route.prototype.dispatch
expected_symbols.why: Invokes matching handlers for a route.

expected_claims.text: createApplication returns an app function that calls app.handle for incoming requests.
app.use calls lazyrouter and delegates middleware registration to router.use.
HTTP verb helpers or app.route create route-specific handlers through this._router.route.
Router.handle walks the router stack and matches layers or paths before route dispatch.
Route.dispatch invokes the matching method handlers for a route.

forbidden_claims.text: Express dispatches requests directly from the application object without using router layers.
Middleware registered with app.use is stored only on the request object.
